title: tests: skip sif test on RHEL
body:
(or, more precisely, if fakeroot binary not in $PATH).

Solves RHEL gating-test failure.

Also, fix rootless cleanup bug (need unshare, to avoid EPERM)

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body: Dan suggested also checking for `squashfs-tools`, but on f35 that only provides binaries in `/usr/sbin` (which is not in default $PATH), and none of those binaries appear in a string search in the skopeo tree anyway, so I'm not sure what a sane check would look like.

> Dan suggested also checking for `squashfs-tools`, but on f35 that only provides binaries in `/usr/sbin` (which is not in default $PATH), and none of those binaries appear in a string search in the skopeo tree anyway

The string I would expect to be found is `"unsquashfs -d %s -f %s && tar --acls --xattrs -C %s -cpf %s ./"`, and AFAICT `/usr/sbin` is in the default `PATH`, at least per https://pagure.io/setup/blob/master/f/profile#_42 . Is there some other mechanism that removes it again?

If `unsquashfs` isn’t in the default path, we should change the transport to find it in the default installation instead of forcing users to deal with that.
Ah yes - I see `unsquashfs` now. I must've fat-fingered it. Anyhow, thanks, if we see further failures I'll add that check.
Thx for running these down to the ground @edsantiago !
